channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Jim Whittaker, the first American to summit Mount Everest, dies at 97
item.description: The celebrated mountaineer, who also served as the first full-time employee of the outdoor retailer REI and later as its president and CEO, died Tuesday at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, his family said.
item.content:encoded:
The celebrated mountaineer, who also served as the first full-time employee of the outdoor retailer REI and later as its president and CEO, died Tuesday at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, his family said.

(Image credit: Jeff Chiu)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Guatemalan man pleads guilty in federal court in crash that killed over 50 in Mexico
item.description: A man pleaded guilty Wednesday in federal court and acknowledged his involvement in an attempt to illegally smuggle migrants to the U.S. when a truck crashed in Mexico in 2021, killing more than 50.
item.content:encoded:
A man pleaded guilty Wednesday in federal court and acknowledged his involvement in an attempt to illegally smuggle migrants to the U.S. when a truck crashed in Mexico in 2021, killing more than 50.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Bill Gates will testify in the Epstein probe; Pam Bondi testimony postponed
item.description: Bill Gates will appear before the House Oversight Committee in June. The Department of Justice said Wednesday that former Attorney General Pam Bondi will not testify for now.
item.content:encoded:
Bill Gates will appear before the House Oversight Committee in June. The Department of Justice said Wednesday that former Attorney General Pam Bondi will not testify for now.

(Image credit: Roy Rochlin)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: A Colorado community is working to keep mobile home pricing affordable
item.description: Mobile home parks are an important source of lower-cost housing. In Durango, Colo., one community fights to keep theirs affordable.
item.content:encoded: Mobile home parks are an important source of lower-cost housing. In Durango, Colo., one community fights to keep theirs affordable.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Skowlund

item.title: Birthplace of civil rights and confederacy cradle: Montgomery is packed with history
item.description: Montgomery, Ala., helped shape America — from the cradle of the Confederacy to the birthplace of the civil rights movement to the city's draw today as the place to reflect on the legacy of slavery.
item.content:encoded: Montgomery, Ala., helped shape America — from the cradle of the Confederacy to the birthplace of the civil rights movement to the city's draw today as the place to reflect on the legacy of slavery.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Elliott

item.title: What has the U.S. war with Iran accomplished?
item.description: Trump's war goals included putting an end to Iran's nuclear program, destroying its military capabilities and creating regime change. That hasn't happened.
item.content:encoded:
Trump's war goals included putting an end to Iran's nuclear program, destroying its military capabilities and creating regime change. That hasn't happened.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Democrats keep doing better in elections since Trump returned to office
item.description: With elections in Georgia and Wisconsin Tuesday, Democrats continued to overperform, which the party started in 2025 when it regularly improved on its margins compared to the presidential race in 2024.
item.content:encoded:
With elections in Georgia and Wisconsin Tuesday, Democrats continued to overperform, which the party started in 2025 when it regularly improved on its margins compared to the presidential race in 2024.

(Image credit: Morry Gash)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Colleges are trying to boost student voting. A Trump probe freezes data for that work
item.description: To figure out how to boost student voting, colleges have relied on a study about campus voter registration and turnout rates. A Trump administration investigation has cut schools off from new data.
item.content:encoded:
To figure out how to boost student voting, colleges have relied on a study about campus voter registration and turnout rates. A Trump administration investigation has cut schools off from new data.

(Image credit: Angela Weiss)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: A fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire shows cracks as attacks continue across the region
item.description: Iran and the U.S. and Israel said they would suspend strikes but countries in the region continued to report attacks and Israel said it would not stop its assault in Lebanon.
item.content:encoded:
Iran and the U.S. and Israel said they would suspend strikes but countries in the region continued to report attacks and Israel said it would not stop its assault in Lebanon.

(Image credit: Anwar Amro)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: ICE acknowledges it is using powerful spyware
item.description: In a letter sent last week, ICE's top official indicated to members of Congress that the agency is using a spyware tool to intercept encrypted messages of fentanyl traffickers.
item.content:encoded:
In a letter sent last week, ICE's top official indicated to members of Congress that the agency is using a spyware tool to intercept encrypted messages of fentanyl traffickers.

(Image credit: Octavio Jones)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block
